contaminant.name: Ceramic Glaze Deposits
contaminant.description: Ceramic-glaze contamination consists of vitrified silicate residues that form during high-temperature firing in ceramic manufacturing, so layer adheres tightly to surfaces after cooling. This contamination shows unique glassy hardness and chemical inertness, which distinguish it from softer organic residues or metallic scales, and on materials like porcelain or alumina, it behaves as a uniform coating that resists cracking under stress. Removal faces challenges from its durable bond, but laser cleaning proves effective because precise energy pulses ablate the glaze selectively, so substrate remains undamaged during treatment.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Silicon Dioxide (crystalline silica) and Lead Oxide generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
